notes: All 184 assembled nodes had valid ids, types (file/function/class/pipeline/config/document) and complexity values (simple/moderate/complex) - the merge script's mechanical fixes were sound.

The 6 dropped 'calls' edges all referenced 5 real functions (resolve_engine_reference, save_config, _is_xcluded, detect_format, translate_task) that genuinely exist in source but were never emitted as sub-nodes by their owning batch. Promoted them to function nodes with default summary/tags/complexity and restored all 6 edges. No dangling edges remain.

Cross-batch import verification: all 44 project-internal import relationships in importMap (18 non-empty entries) already have matching 'imports' edges. Nothing to recover - the script's 'recovered 0' was correct.

Data quality: batches under-emit callee functions (only 54 function nodes for the call graph that references them). Consider prompting batches to always emit any function named as a call target. Not an upstream id-correction issue - just missing sub-node coverage.
